title: Asymptotic CI Validation: Simulation Results
text: article presents simulation-based validation asymptotic confidence interval (CI) methods available RESI package via resi_pe_asymptotic() resi(). asymptotic methods share Σ̂R\hat\Sigma_R estimator — extended influence-function approach treats sandwich bread Σ̂XA\hat\Sigma_{XA} meat Σ̂XB\hat\Sigma_{XB} independent estimators (argument deriv_method = "extended" internally) — differ invert asymptotic distribution Wald statistic T2=nS̃2T^2 = n\tilde{S}^2. Results shown four estimation settings: background RESI, see RESI package website Jones et al. (2025).

title: Asymptotic CI Validation: Simulation Results
text: evaluations use plasmode simulation design (Gadbury et al. 2008) built around insurance dataset (N=1338N = 1338) shipped package. Bootstrap resampling (replacement) full dataset gives empirical sampling distribution. RESI values estimated full dataset via resi_pe() serve population targets. Two models evaluated: Linear model (lm): log10(charges) ~ ns(age, df=3) * sex + bmi + smoker + region Logistic model (glm, binomial): (charges > 15000) ~ ns(age, df=3) * sex + bmi + smoker + region simulation cell (model × variance estimator × sample size) uses 1 000 replicates. Results shown sample sizes n∈{50,100,200,500,1000,2000,5000}n \\{50, 100, 200, 500, 1000, 2000, 5000\}.

title: Asymptotic CI Validation: Simulation Results
text: model coefficient ANOVA term following quantities reported: Coverage: proportion replicates 95% CI contains population RESI value. Width: median CI width. Bias MSE: mean signed mean squared deviation Ŝ\hat{S} population value. Bootstrap CIs (1 000 replicates, non-parametric) provide reference standard.

what: RESI: Robust Effect Size Inference in R
title: RESI: Robust Effect Size Inference in R
text: RESI R package designed implement Robust Effect Size Index (RESI, denoted S) described Vandekar, Tao, & Blume (2020). RESI versatile effect size measure can easily computed added common reports (summary ANOVA tables). package currently supports lm, glm, nls, survreg, coxph, hurdle, zeroinfl, gee, geeglm, lme, lmerMod, lmrob, glmrob models. Confidence intervals now computed using bootstrap one three asymptotic methods: profiled quadratic form, Cornish-Fisher expansion, normal approximation. Bayesian bootstrap also available lm nls models. addition main resi function, package also includes point-estimate-function (resi_pe), conversions S common effect size measures vice versa, print methods, plot methods, summary methods, Anova/anova methods. like contribute package, please branch GitHub submit pull request describing contribution. Please use GitHub Issues page report problems Discussions page seek additional support.

Default c(50, 100, 200, 500, 1000, 2000, 5000). nboot Integer, bootstrap replicates per internal resi call. Default 500. Use 10 initial testing. Ignored ci.method != "boot". alpha Numeric, CI significance level. Default 0.05. ci.method Character, CI method passed resi. One "boot" (bootstrap, default), "normal" (asymptotic truncated-normal), "qf" (asymptotic quadratic-form / Imhof). ci.method != "boot", nboot ignored. output.dir Character, path directory results saved. Created exist. Defaults "resiBootSim", "resiAsympNormalSim", "resiAsympQFSim" based ci.method NULL. fixed.knots Logical. TRUE, spline knots fixed empirical tertiles age full insurance dataset rather re-selected df = 3 bootstrap sample. Default FALSE. mc.cores.settings Integer, cores outer mclapply (setting \(\times\) sample size) combinations. Default 1. mc.cores.reps Integer, cores inner mclapply simulation replicates within (setting, n) cell. Default 1.

title: Insurance Plasmode Simulation for RESI Evaluation — insurancePlasmodeSim

title: Insurance Plasmode Simulation for RESI Evaluation — insurancePlasmodeSim
text: Two models evaluated: lm: log10(charges) ~ ns(age, df=3) * sex + bmi + smoker + region glm: (charges > 10000) ~ ns(age, df=3) * sex + bmi + smoker + region     family = binomial() model evaluated parametric (vcovfunc = stats::vcov) robust (vcovfunc = sandwich::vcovHC) variance settings, yielding four simulation conditions. Parallelization via mclapply, uses forking supported Windows (falls back sequential evaluation Windows).

title: Robust Effect Size Index (RESI) point and interval estimation for models — resi
text: model.full lm, glm, nls, survreg, coxph, hurdle, zeroinfl, gee, geeglm lme model object. ... Ignored. model.reduced Fitted model object type model.full. default `NULL`; model full model intercept. data Data.frame object coercible data.frame model.full data (required model types). anova Logical, whether produce Anova table RESI columns added. default = `TRUE`. coefficients Logical, whether produce coefficients (summary) table RESI columns added. default = `TRUE`. overall Logical, whether produce overall Wald test comparing full reduced model RESI columns added. default = `TRUE`. nboot Numeric, number bootstrap replicates. Used `ci.method = "boot"`. default, 1000. boot.method String, type bootstrap use: `nonparam` = non-parametric bootstrap (default); `bayes` = Bayesian bootstrap. vcovfunc variance estimator function constructing Wald test statistic. default, vcovHC (robust (sandwich) variance estimator). alpha Numeric, significance level constructed CIs. default, 0.05. store.boot Logical, whether store bootstrapped estimates. default, `FALSE`. Anova.args List, additional arguments passed Anova function. vcov.args List, additional arguments passed vcovfunc. unbiased Logical, whether use unbiased alternative T/Z statistic RESI conversion. default, `TRUE`. See details. parallel See documentation boot. ncpus See documentation boot. long Logical, whether data longitudinal/clustered. default, `FALSE`. clvar Character, name cluster/id variable data clustered. default, `NULL`. ci.method Character, method used compute confidence intervals. One `"boot"` (bootstrap), `"qf"` (quadratic-form inversion, default lm glm), `"normal"` (normal approximation), `"cf"` (Cornish-Fisher inversion). See `resi_pe_asymptotic` details asymptotic methods.

specify additional arguments to the variance-covariance function via vcov.args   resi_obj = resi(mod, data = RESI::insurance, alpha = 0.01,     vcov.args = list(type = "HC0"), nboot = 25)   summary(resi_obj)   car::Anova(resi_obj)} #> Analysis of Deviance Table (Type II tests) #>  #> Response: smoker #>                          Df  Chisq Pr(>Chisq)     RESI 0.5%    99.5% #> splines::ns(age, df = 3)  3 1.4735    0.68841 0.000000    0 0.089389 #> region                    3 7.2960    0.06304 0.056663    0 0.135462   ## RESI on a survival model with alternate Z2S if(requireNamespace("survival")){   # fit coxph model on example data from survival package   # Note: for survival models, you need to specify robust variance in the model   # creation. resi will ignore the vcovfunc argument for this reason.   mod.coxph =  survival::coxph(survival::Surv(time, status) ~ age + sex + wt.loss,    data=survival::lung, robust = TRUE)    # run resi on the model   # to use the alternative Z to RESI formula (which is equal in absolute value to the   # chi-square to RESI (S) formula), specify unbiased = FALSE.   resi(mod.coxph, data = survival::lung, unbiased = FALSE, nboot = 10)} #> Loading required namespace: survival #>  #> Analysis of effect sizes based on RESI: #> Confidence level =  0.05 #> Call:  survival::coxph(formula = survival::Surv(time, status) ~ age +  #>     sex + wt.loss, data = survival::lung, robust = TRUE) #>  #> Coefficient Table  #>         Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    RESI    2.5%   97.5% #> age       0.0201     0.0101  1.9915   0.0464  0.1177  0.0082  0.3016 #> sex      -0.5210     0.1670 -3.1202   0.0018 -0.2020 -0.3004 -0.0520 #> wt.loss   0.0008     0.0060  0.1264   0.8994  0.0000 -0.0827  0.0835 #>  #>  #> Analysis of Deviance Table (Type II tests) #>  #> Response: survival::Surv(time, status) #>         Df  Chisq Pr(>Chisq)   RESI   2.5%  97.5% #> age      1 3.9662     0.0464 0.1177 0.0082 0.3016 #> sex      1 9.7357     0.0018 0.2020 0.0520 0.3004 #> wt.loss  1 0.0160     0.8994 0.0000 0.0000 0.1021 #>  #> Overall RESI comparing model to intercept-only model: #>  #>      chi2 df      P   RESI   2.5%  97.5% #> 1 13.7717  3 0.0032 0.2244 0.0994 0.3694 #>  #> Notes: #> 1. The RESI was calculated using a robust covariance estimator. #> 2. Confidence intervals (CIs) constructed using 10 non-parametric bootstraps.  #>

text: Runs plasmode simulation verify asymptotic normal CI machinery correctly calibrated four model settings (lm/glm x parametric/robust).  (setting, sample size) cell function checks: Bias(theta): \(\hat\theta \\theta_{\rm true}\) –     raw coefficients (\(\phi = \hat\sigma^2\) lm) converge     full-dataset values. vcov check (estimator consistency):     \(n \cdot \bar V_{\rm analytic} / (n_{\rm full} \cdot V_{\rm true}) \1\). vcov check B (calibration):     \(n \cdot \widehat{\rm Var}(\hat\beta) / (n_{\rm full} \cdot V_{\rm true}) \1\). Bias(R): RESI point estimates converge full-dataset     values. sigma2S check (estimator consistency):     \(\bar{\hat\sigma}^2_S / \sigma^2_{S,\rm true} \1\). sigma2S check B (CI calibration):     \(n \cdot \widehat{\rm Var}(\hat R) / \sigma^2_{S,\rm true} \1\). True values taken full insurance dataset using definitions insurancePlasmodeSim.  \(\sigma^2_S\) extracted asymptotic-normal CI half-width: \(\hat\sigma^2_S = n \cdot ({\rm hw}/z_{\alpha/2})^2\).

title: Compute the robust effect size index estimate from t statistic (default) — t2S
text: function computes S, RESI, t statistic. formula unbiased estimator (default) derived solving expected value t statistic S. unbiased consistent. formula unbiased conversion : \(S = (t * \sqrt(2) * \Gamma(rdf/2)) / (\sqrt(n * rdf) * \Gamma((rdf - 1)/2))\) formula alternative estimator derived squaring t statistic using f2S formula. estimator may appealing intuitive relationship F statistic; absolute value RESI estimates using formula equal RESI estimate using F statistic model. However, estimator finite sample bias, important consideration coverage bootstrapping resi uses. formula alternative conversion : \( \sqrt(max(0, (t^2 * (rdf - 2)/rdf - 1)/rdf))\)

title: Compute the robust effect size index estimate from Z statistic — z2S
text: function computes S, RESI, Z statistic. formula unbiased estimator (default) derived solving expected value Z statistic S. unbiased consistent. formula unbiased conversion : \(S = Z/\sqrt(n)\) formula alternative estimator derived squaring Z statistic using chisq2S formula. estimator may appealing intuitive relationship Chi-square statistic; absolute value RESI estimates using formula equal RESI estimate using Chi-square statistic model. title: RESI 1.4.2
text: Added analytic confidence intervals based quadratic form, Cornish-Fisher, Normal approximations. qf now default confidence interval method lm glm. Added support robustbase models (lmrob glmrob) via new resi_pe.lmrob, resi_pe.glmrob, resi.lmrob, resi.glmrob methods. default vcovfunc = stats::vcov, uses model’s built-robust sandwich variance. glmrob redirects sandwich::vcovHC stats::vcov warning since vcovHC support glmrob (#12).

title: RESI 1.4.2
text: Fixed error passing data argument containing NAs resi() GEE models (geeglm). Rows NA model variable now silently stripped bootstrapping, matching complete cases used fit model preventing cluster-size mismatches re-fitting (#51). Fixed misleading error summary.resi() different alpha level requested confidence intervals recomputed. message now clearly explains either () bootstrapping supported model type, (b) resi() run store.boot = TRUE, instructs user re-run resi() desired alpha directly (#53). Fixed resi() failing compute overall Wald test model response computed expression (e.g. log10(charges) (charges > 10000)). Previously intercept-reduced model fitted inside forked parallel workers update() tried re-evaluate expression environment underlying variable scope. fix constructs reduced-model formula using already-evaluated column name model.frame(), avoiding re-evaluation. Added informative error vcov.args = list(type = "const") passed together vcovfunc = sandwich::vcovHC. message explains type = "const" OLS sandwich (robust) directs users use vcovfunc = stats::vcov parametric variance estimation instead (#50). resi_pe now reports CS-RESI L-RESI lmer.
